rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MATTIE TODD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/1/26. (No earlier availability.) No response to the issue posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 3/9/26. (1) No proof of service has been filed yet of the correct Notice of Hearing mailed at least 15 days before this hearing. (2) The proof of service filed on 2/24/26 showed Lorenzo Thorn's address with the wrong apartment number. (3) The attorney's declaration filed on 4/8/26 (no courtesy copy was received by the examiner) indicates different addresses again for Pasha Thorn and Kathleena Thorn, both in Antioch, CA, not Minooka, IL, or San Bruno, CA, respectively, as shown on the proof of service filed on 2/24/26. The petitioner's verified supplement, setting forth the correct, current addresses for these three heirs, must be filed. Proof of service of 15 days' notice to them (or waivers of notice by them) of the hearing on 6/1/26 must be filed. A courtesy copy of the supplement and the proof of service must be received by the assigned examiner by 5/22/26 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/18/2026. The following issues need to be cleared before 5/8/2026. 1. Notice of Hearing to be directly sent to Jennifer C. In c/o her counsel seems insufficient. CRC 7.51(a)(2). 2. The petition requests reimbursement of $5,124.50 to the personal representative as she paid the amount out of her pocket to Attorney Domingo for the appeal. If court approval is sought for extraordinary services (which appears to be here) related to the appeal, fee declarations detailing these services are required. See CRC 7.702 and 7.703. 3. Proof of service of the fee declaration to the beneficiaries.

Neither a petition for final distribution nor a report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate has been filed yet, as ordered on 9/16/24. Section 8800(b).] It is not necessary to receive confirmation from the FTB about taxes in order to file an I&A. 3. A courtesy copy of the declaration filed on 4/13/26 has not been received by this examiner as of 16:44 on 4/14/26. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 2010 KATHARINE LYNCH TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Suspend Trustee, Bring Trust Under Court Supervision
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/01/2026: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing, absent a waiver signed by each person entitled to notice. 2) If petitioner intended to request his appointment as general successor trustee, as so indicated in the proposed order, the petition caption and prayer for relief must be amended to so state. See CRC 7.102. New 30-day notice of an amendment to the petition (or an amended petition) must be given (see CRC 7.50 and 7.53(a)) on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 with a copy of the remote appearance information attached thereto, after the amendemnt or amended petition has been accepted by the clerk (see LRSF 14.14(A)). 3) A verified allegation must be made to state the market value of personal property of the trust and estimated annual gross income from both personal property and real property of the trust, as set forth in the proposed order, for the Court to fix petitioner's bond as trustee.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSARIO ABILO ABANTAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/11/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Item 8 of the petition is not entirely clear. Is Jose Maria Abantao, who waived bond and declined to serve, the same as Jose Abantao, who post-deceased? Was he the trustee of the Abantao Living Trust, whose trustee is now Veronica Abantao? (3) How are the two sons of post-deceased son Ray Abilo Abantao his successors-in-interest? Did Ray die intestate? Was there no personal representative appointed for his estate? Did he not have a trust? (4) Decedent's son John Abilo Abantao and Ray Abilo Abantao's two children do not appear to have waived bond. (5) Whose child is the petitioner? Please provide a courtesy copy of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/27/26. Lack of courtesy copies delays review. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

[LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Please file a copy of the vesting deed to show how title to the real property is held. (4) The will is not self-proving. Proof of Subscribing Witness must be filed.
